title: Postgres: Too Many Clients Already
description:
## The Situation

After provisioning a new Postgres instance with 'optimized' settings, your app started intermittently failing under load. The error is clear:

```
FATAL: sorry, too many clients already
```

Your application pool is configured for 25 connections. But at any load above a trickle, connections are refused.

## Your Task

1. Run the stress test to observe the failure
2. Find the Postgres configuration responsible
3. Fix it and restart Postgres
4. Confirm all 15 concurrent workers connect successfully

## Useful Commands

```bash
# Observe the failure
python3 /home/user/stress_test.py

# Connect to postgres directly
psql -U appuser -d appdb

# Inside psql:
SHOW max_connections;
SELECT count(*) FROM pg_stat_activity;
SELECT * FROM pg_stat_activity;
\q

# Find and edit the config
ps aux | grep postgres | head -3
find /etc/postgresql -name postgresql.conf
nano /etc/postgresql/*/main/postgresql.conf

# Apply and restart
service postgresql restart

# Re-run the stress test
python3 /home/user/stress_test.py
```
hints: The error 'too many clients already' is Postgres refusing new connections because it's at its configured limit — not because connections are leaking.
Run SHOW max_connections; inside psql to see the hard cap. Then check how many connections your app pool expects. Spot the mismatch.
Edit max_connections in /etc/postgresql/*/main/postgresql.conf. Set it to 100 or higher (remember: 3 are reserved for superusers). Then: service postgresql restart.
